sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 5.28 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.5)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a group and$H$a torsion-free subgroup of$G$such that the augmentation ideal$I_G$of the integral group-ring$G$can be decomposed as$I_G=I_H G M$for some$G$-sub\-mo\-dule$M$. Prove that$G$is a free product of the form$G = H*K$. { D.\,E.\,Cohen} This has been proved (W.\,Dicks, M.\,J.\,Dunwoody, Groups acting on graphs, Cambridge Univ. Press, Cambridge--New York, 1989).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
